J.reasoning: All others contribute 0, so contributing more only reduces your kept tokens while increasing share slightly (0.3 per token of contribution). Net effect is 5 - 0.7*c, maximal at c=0.

I should contribute 0 to avoid reducing my own tokens while the pool is funded by others (one person aims 0, another 1). Without knowing others' exact plans, maximizing own payoff favors not contributing.

Each extra token contributed reduces my payoff by 0.7 (5 - c + 0.3*(c+S) = 5 - 0.7c + 0.3S). Since 0.7 per token is lost and the pool gain is only 0.3 per token, contributing any amount lowers my final payout. Therefore, contribute 0.
Assuming others contribute 3 total; payoff = (5-c) + 0.3*(3+c) = 5.9 - 0.7c, maximized at c=0.
Contributing any amount lowers your net payoff since each extra contribution increases your share by 0.3 but costs 1, so net -0.7 per token. Therefore best to contribute 0 regardless of others.
Other players contribute a total of 5; since final payout = (5 - c) + 0.3*(c+5) = 6.5 - 0.7c, contributing 0 maximizes the payoff.
Contributing 0 maximizes payoff since your payoff is 5 - 0.7c + 0.3*sum_other; the coefficient of c is negative, so minimizing c (to 0) yields the highest total.
Given S=7 from others, payout = 5 - 0.7c + 0.3*7; coefficient on c is -0.7, so contributing 0 maximizes payoff.
Contributing reduces personal payoff by 0.7 per token since your share increases only by 0.3 per contributed token, so best to contribute nothing.
Your payoff = 5 - c_i + 0.3*S, where S = c_i + sum_others. This is 5 - 0.7*c_i + 0.3*sum_others, so it decreases as you contribute more. Therefore contribute 0 to maximize payout.
With others contributing 10 total, your final payout is 8 - 0.7*x, so it is maximized by contributing 0.
Total contribution = J + 11. J's payoff = (5 - J) + 0.3*(J + 11) = 8.3 - 0.7J, which decreases with J. Hence contribute 0 to maximize payoff.
Given others (Q+T+Z) contribute 12 and M contributes 0, your payoff is (5 - x) + 0.3*(x + 12) = 8.6 - 0.7x. This decreases with x, so contribute 0.

With 5 players, final payoff = 5 - 0.7x + 0.3*S_others. Since the coefficient of x is negative, contributing 0 maximizes payoff. Given others_total is 13, not contributing yields the highest final payout.

In a public goods game, your payoff is maximized by contributing nothing: increasing your contribution always lowers your own payoff by 0.7 per token, while others' contributions (S) add only 0.3 per token to your share. Therefore, c=0 is optimal given any S.
With others contributing 15 in total and the pool being shared after 1.5x, my payoff decreases as I contribute more, so contributing 0 maximizes my final payout.
With others contributing 16 total, my payoff is maximized by contributing 0 since each extra token I contribute reduces my kept tokens by 1 and only increases pool share by 0.3, net -0.7 per token.
With others contributing 17 tokens total, the pool becomes 25.5; each gets 5.1. My payoff is (5 - c) + 5.1, which is maximized at c=0.
Total contributions from others are fixed at 18. Final payout = (5 - x) + 1.5*(x+18)/5 = 10.4 - 0.7x, maximized at x=0. Therefore contribute 0.
Others are contributing a large total (as announced). Reducing your contribution keeps more tokens and yields a larger personal share from the 1.5x pool, so x=0 maximizes payoff.
Total other contributions are 20; pool share is 0.3*(J+20) and kept tokens are 5-J. Payoff = 11 - 0.7J, minimized J increases payoff, so contribute 0.
Assuming others contribute 1 token in total and the rest 0, your payoff is 5.3 - 0.7x, which is maximized by x=0. Therefore contribute 0.

With a fixed sum from others, your final payout decreases with each additional token you contribute. Contributing 0 maximizes your kept tokens and yields a share that still benefits from others' contributions.

In a 5-player public goods game with 1.5x multiplier and equal sharing, each contributed token yields only 0.3 return to you. Since your own contribution costs 1 token but only returns 0.3 to you, your best response is to contribute 0.

In a 5-player linear public goods game with payoff 5 - c + 0.3*(sum of others) + 0.3*c, contributing more than 0 lowers your payoff. Since you can’t influence others’ contributions and the best response is to contribute nothing, choose 0.
Contributing nothing maximizes your payoff: your payoff = 5 - x + 0.3(x + C) = 5 - 0.7x + 0.3C, so increasing x lowers your payoff. Contribute 0 regardless of others.
